channel.title: Frontion - Geopolitical News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Trump heads to G7 summit after announcing US-Iran peace agreement
item.description: President Trump travels to the French Alps for the G7 summit fresh off declaring a peace deal with Iran, with Ukraine war and trade tensions also on the agenda.

item.title: G7 leaders convene in France after US and Iran declare agreement to end war
item.description: Leaders of the Group of Seven wealthy nations gather at Evian-les-Bains to discuss next steps on Iran, Ukraine, and global trade following the US-Iran peace deal announcement.

item.title: Anti-G7 protesters torch Tesla and smash UN office windows in Geneva
item.description: Geneva police fired teargas at protesters who set fire to a Tesla vehicle and smashed windows at a UN agency during violent demonstrations ahead of the G7 summit across the border in France.

item.title: Iran and US agree framework deal to end war as Trump says Strait of Hormuz will reopen
item.description: Pakistan announced a ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran that will end military operations on all fronts, with the strategic Strait of Hormuz set to reopen following formal signing.

item.title: G7 summit opens in France with Ukraine war and new US-Iran agreement on agenda
item.description: The G7 summit kicks off in Evian-les-Bains with the freshly announced US-Iran peace deal and the ongoing Ukraine war dominating discussions among world leaders.

item.title: China AI stocks diverge as Zhipu soars while Minimax stumbles
item.description: A widening valuation gap between China's two top LLM startups signals investor分化tion in the Chinese AI sector, with Zhipu gaining while Minimax loses ground.

item.title: European aviation fuel supply tightens as Iran war cuts imports to 80%
item.description: European aviation fuel imports remain at just 80% of pre-war levels despite US-Iran peace deal, with high prices and potential flight reductions expected in autumn.

item.title: Singapore retrenchments rise sharply among degree holders and older workers
item.description: Q1 2026 saw increased retrenchments in Singapore with the steepest increase among higher-educated workers, reflecting ongoing business restructuring in the city-state.

item.title: Japan to resume domestic LNG carrier construction by 2035
item.description: Three major Japanese shipbuilders plan to restart domestic LNG carrier construction by 2035, seeking technology cooperation from Korean shipyards after years without production.

item.title: Oil Slides $4 as US-Iran Peace Deal Reopens Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Oil prices dropped sharply after the US and Iran announced a tentative peace agreement that includes reopening the strategic Strait of Hormuz, easing concerns about global energy supplies.

item.title: Taiwan Fires US-Supplied HIMARS Rockets Toward China in First
item.description: Taiwan fired rockets from US-supplied HIMARS mobile launch systems toward China in a significant military exercise, showcasing its defensive capabilities amid escalating tensions with Beijing.

item.title: North Korea Declares Nuclear Status Irreversible, Rejects Denuclearisation
item.description: North Korea stated that its nuclear weapons status is now irreversible and that denuclearisation is 'terminated', marking a significant shift in its diplomatic stance.

item.title: OPEC Lowers 2026 Global Oil Demand Growth Forecast
item.description: OPEC cut its 2026 oil demand growth forecast for the second time, citing economic uncertainty and the impact of the Middle East conflict on global energy markets.

item.title: Russian Attack Sets Kyiv's Centuries-Old Monastery Ablaze, Kills Five
item.description: Russian drone strikes damaged Kyiv's historic Pechersk Lavra monastery and killed five people in a major overnight attack, Ukrainian officials said.

item.title: Putin Sends Birthday Greetings to Trump, Praises Him as Bright and Remarkable
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin extended birthday greetings to Donald Trump on his 80th birthday, describing him as a "bright, remarkable person and politician" and suggesting the two leaders could raise their countries ties to a new level.

item.title: Japanese Shippers Await Details on Hormuz Reopening as Mines Remain Threat
item.description: Japanese shipping companies welcomed the US-Iran peace agreement that will reopen the Strait of Hormuz but are awaiting more details on mine clearance before allowing vessels to pass through the vital chokepoint.

item.title: UK Prime Minister Starmer Expected to Announce Social Media Ban for Young Teenagers
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is expected to announce a social media ban for young teenagers, with proposals reportedly going further than Australias similar restrictions. The move aims to protect childrens online wellbeing.

item.title: Macron Frames Évian G7 Agenda Hoping Trump Will Stay for Full Summit
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron is hosting his final G7 summit in Évian, with the agenda focused on ending the Ukraine war and pushing for resolutions in Gaza and Iran. Leaders hope Trump will remain for the entire meeting after leaving the last gathering early.

item.title: Global Leaders React to US-Iran Peace Agreement with Cautious Optimism
item.description: The United States and Iran have agreed to a peace deal and an end to military operations on all fronts, with European leaders warning that Tehran must never acquire a nuclear weapon. The preliminary agreement is expected to be signed Friday in Switzerland.

item.title: Russia suspected of using Swedish church near airport for espionage activities
item.description: Swedish intelligence services suspect Moscow is using a Russian Orthodox church near Vasteras airport as a platform for espionage and hybrid activities, warning it could threaten national security.

item.title: Only 25% of F-35 fleet fully mission capable as readiness rates decline
item.description: A GAO report reveals the F-35's full mission capable rate fell to 25% through fiscal 2025, with readiness rates continuing to decline amid ongoing maintenance and sustainment challenges.

item.title: Russia building major troop deployment infrastructure along NATO's Northern Flank
item.description: Russia is constructing facilities for tens of thousands of troops near the Arctic, while NATO bolsters defenses with a new Arctic combat force as tensions rise on the alliance's northern border.

item.title: Germany and Spain launch 'Team Gen 6' after FCAS fighter program collapses
item.description: Germany and Spain are forming a new coalition to develop sixth-generation fighter technology after the Franco-German FCAS program collapsed over irreconcilable differences on requirements.

item.title: Twelve Killed in Missouri Skydiving Plane Crash
item.description: A plane carrying a pilot and 11 passengers on a skydiving outing crashed near Butler Memorial Airport in Missouri, killing all aboard. The aircraft caught fire shortly after takeoff around 11:30 AM local time.

item.title: G7 Summit Begins in France as Anti-G7 Protests Erupt in Geneva
item.description: World leaders convened for the G7 summit in France as thousands of protesters clashed with police in Geneva. Swiss authorities deployed water cannons and tear gas to disperse demonstrators ahead of the meeting.
